name: Midjourney V8.1
summary: image gen update — HD mode 3x faster and 3x cheaper than V8.0, standard renders 50% faster and 25% cheaper, brings back image prompts and adds a Prompt Shortener; live on midjourney.com and Discord

name: Cursor SDK
summary: TypeScript SDK that turns Cursor's coding agent into programmable infrastructure — npm install @cursor/sdk, run agents locally or on Cursor's sandboxed cloud VMs from CI, backend services, or your own product, billed per-token

name: Amazon Quick
summary: always-on desktop AI assistant that watches across your Google Workspace, Zoom, Airtable, Dropbox, and Teams and surfaces what needs attention — free tier, sign up with a personal Google/Apple/GitHub email, no AWS account required

name: NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Nano Omni
summary: open multimodal model unifying vision, audio, and language → text with 9x higher throughput than other open omni models — weights live on Hugging Face and OpenRouter, also via NVIDIA NIM

name: DeepSeek V4
summary: open-source flagship in two sizes (V4-Pro 1.6T params, V4-Flash 284B) with 1M token context and Hybrid Attention — preview weights on Hugging Face, API at $0.28/M tokens for Flash and $3.48/M for Pro

name: GPT-5.5
summary: OpenAI's new flagship — better at coding, deep research, and multi-step agentic tasks; rolling out to Plus/Pro/Business in ChatGPT and Codex, with GPT-5.5 and 5.5 Pro live in the API

name: Qwen3.6-27B
summary: Apache-2.0 dense 27B coding model that beats Qwen's prior 397B MoE on SWE-bench Verified (77.2) and matches Claude 4.5 Opus on Terminal-Bench — small enough to run on a single GPU, weights on Hugging Face

name: GPT Image 2
summary: OpenAI's most capable image gen yet — near-perfect multilingual text rendering, surgical multi-turn edits, 4K resolution (beta), and strong layout for slides, infographics, and maps; live in the API, Codex, and ChatGPT
